commands.review_summary:
The PR removes browser-specific static moderation rules and reason codes, updates tests to expect clean static scans for those cases, and changes acceptable-use/spec wording to say SkillSpector owns browser-automation analysis.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a security policy/scanner ownership change, not a reported runtime bug. The diff and updated tests show the new static scanner behavior directly.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human security/policy review should decide whether acceptable-use policy should keep explicit anti-bot/CAPTCHA/stealth-scraping examples before merge.

target.title: Delegate browser automation scanning to SkillSpector
target.body:
## Summary
- Remove ClawHub static-scanner rules and reason codes for browser automation, stealth browser abuse, browser credential automation, browser file rendering, and browser typing.
- Keep static moderation focused on non-browser-specific concrete source/sink evidence because SkillSpector already owns browser-automation analysis internally.
- Update acceptable-use and security-moderation docs so scraping/stealth browser guidance is not duplicated as ClawHub static policy.

## Why
This removes duplicated browser-automation moderation logic from ClawHub. SkillSpector covers this analysis lane, so ClawHub should not independently classify stealth browsers, anti-bot/crawling behavior, or browser automation patterns in the static scanner.

## Production note
Restored `scrapling-official` after diagnostics showed D4Vinci was not account-banned and the skill had a clean moderation verdict with legacy `user.moderation` state.

Linear: CLAW-252

## Verification
- `bun test convex/lib/moderationEngine.test.ts -t "browser"`
- `bun test convex/lib/moderationEngine.test.ts`
- `bun run ci:unit`
- `bun run ci:static`
target.author: patrick-erichsen
